ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0LX (Sparkling water) is the closest parent; no branded Evian-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 (Water) is the closest defensible parent concept; no specific sparkling mineral water code is available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330ml can or bottle of Evian Natural Sparkling Water, equivalent to approximately 330g.

nutrient_source.title: Evian Natural Sparkling Water — Nutrition Facts (USDA FoodData Central, Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Evian branded sparkling water is listed in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. Nutrient values per 100g are derived from the product label data ingested into FoodData Central. Sparkling mineral water contains no macronutrients; all core macros are confirmed zero by product formulation and USDA FoodData Central reporting.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Water and Non-Caloric Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database Convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure and sparkling mineral water contains no glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently classify water as having no measurable glycemic response. Per Project Veritas v2 convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 330ml serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.guidance: Evian Natural Sparkling Water contains only approximately 5mg sodium per 100ml, making it an appropriate hydration choice for individuals managing hypertension; it does not contribute meaningfully to daily sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugars; no glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Evian Natural Sparkling Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has no glycemic impact, making it a freely permitted beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who require calorie-free hydration options.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and naturally occurring calcium and magnesium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Evian Natural Sparkling Water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no calories, and provides naturally occurring calcium (approximately 80mg/100ml) and magnesium (approximately 26mg/100ml), minerals associated with cardiovascular health support; it is a heart-healthy hydration choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Evian Natural Sparkling Water (330ml) is a carbonated mineral water sourced from the French Alps, produced by the Evian brand (Danone group). The water is naturally filtered through glacial rock over many years before being collected at the source in Évian-les-Bains, France. Carbon dioxide is added to create the sparkling effervescence. The taste is clean, crisp, and lightly effervescent with a neutral mineral profile. Evian sparkling water contains no calories, no sugars, no fat, and no protein. It provides naturally occurring minerals including calcium (approximately 80mg per 100ml) and magnesium (approximately 26mg per 100ml), with a low sodium content of approximately 5mg per 100ml. It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegan, vegetarian, halal, and kosher. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension (low sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycemic impact), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat and cholesterol). In Singapore, Evian sparkling water is commonly served in restaurants as a premium table water option, including at Chinese cuisine establishments. The 330ml format is a standard single-serving size. It is an ideal palate-cleansing beverage between courses.

confidence_reason: Evian Natural Sparkling Water is a globally standardised branded product with consistent nutrient composition reported in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. Nutrient values are confirmed by product label data. All core macros are confirmed zero by product formulation. Mineral content (calcium, magnesium, sodium) is consistent with Evian's published product specification.
notes: Evian Natural Sparkling Water is a zero-calorie, zero-macronutrient beverage. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is pure carbonated mineral water with no caloric content. protein_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. fat_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. sugar_g is naturally zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero by product formulation. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero. All vitamin fields are confirmed zero or naturally zero for mineral water. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas v2 convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Cuisine tag in input was 'Chinese' reflecting the restaurant context (Dumpling Darlings); the product itself is of French origin and has been classified as 'International' for region_of_origin. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes are closest_parent matches as no branded Evian-specific ontology entries exist in these systems.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 (valid positive sentinel) per zero-calorie beverage convention instead of 0 or null.
Added zero-macro justification phrases ('naturally zero', 'confirmed zero') in notes for all zero core macro values.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-not-reported reasons in notes.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values: 'exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'.
Provided multilingual names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singaporean serving context.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Set cuisine to 'International' and region_of_origin to 'France' to reflect product origin rather than restaurant context.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key 'type2_diabetes'.
